votes.pillar: Asset quality is STRUCTURALLY improving (GS3 down to 2.88%, credit cost falling)
votes.reasoning: The ratio improvement is real but modest and the word "structurally" is not supported by audited data; the "credit cost falling" half is outright contradicted. (1) GS3 of 2.88% is a STANDALONE figure only — audited consolidated GS3/NS3/CRAR are "Not Applicable at consolidated level" (audited p.9/p.22), so the headline mixes a standalone ratio into a consolidated bull case. (2) GS3 fell just ~41bps YoY (Emkay trend: 3.29% Q4FY25 to 2.88% Q4FY26) on a loan book that grew +25.6% (93,773 to 117,821cr, audited p.9) — a larger denominator mechanically dilutes the ratio, and NS3 actually ROSE QoQ from 0.92% to 0.96% (+4bps, Emkay). (3) "Credit cost falling" rests on a FLAT impairment line (2,184.10 vs 2,193.35cr, audited p.8) — but the net loss on derecognition (write-offs) DOUBLED to 561.77cr (FY25 275.04). True combined credit cost ROSE +11.2% to 2,745.87cr; on average loans that is 2.60% combined vs 2.06% impairment-only — a 54bp gap hidden below the impairment line. So the audited combined credit cost went UP, not down. (4) The QoQ step-down (3.19% to 2.88%) coincided with a one-time ECL model refresh that released 301cr of overlays and CUT the Stage-3 PCR from 73-74% to ~67-68% (Emkay PCR 71.95% to 67.30%, -464bps; implied standalone Stage-3 PCR 66.7%). Reducing coverage on a stressed book is the opposite of structural quality improvement. Heavy write-offs (the 562cr derecognition loss) also strip Stage-3 loans off the book, holding the reported ratio artificially low — ARC sales were immaterial (127.52cr principal, sold above NBV), so write-offs, not recoveries, are the lever. The favourable trend (collection efficiency back to 99.8%, slowing roll-forwards) is MANAGEMENT-CLAIMED (transcript), not yet evidenced in the audited combined credit cost, which still rose.
votes.strongest_refutation: Audited p.8: the headline "flat impairment" (2,184.10 vs 2,193.35cr) is offset by the net loss on derecognition (write-offs) DOUBLING to 561.77cr (FY25 275.04cr). Combined true credit cost therefore ROSE +11.2% to 2,745.87cr (2.60% of avg loans vs 2.06% impairment-only). The audited combined credit cost went UP YoY — directly contradicting "credit cost falling" — and the doubled write-offs are precisely what mechanically holds reported GS3 at 2.88%.
votes.what_would_confirm: A write-off-adjusted, full-cycle view showing the combined (impairment + derecognition) credit cost trending DOWN — not just the impairment line — across multiple periods; absolute gross Stage-3 balances (rupee amount, not just the ratio) FALLING despite +25.6% loan growth; Stage-3 PCR HELD or rising rather than cut from 73% to 68%; gross slippage/roll-forward time series and Stage-2 balances; and audited (not management-reported) consolidated GS3/NS3 — none of which exist in the current results pack, which discloses asset quality only at the standalone level and only as point-in-time ratios. The FY26 annual report's ECL movement schedule (Stage 1/2/3 reconciliation, gross carrying amounts, write-offs, and the impact of the model-refresh overlay release) would be needed to confirm whether the improvement is structural or accounting/denominator-driven.

votes.pillar: Asset quality is STRUCTURALLY improving (GS3 down to 2.88%, credit cost falling)
votes.reasoning: The headline ratios improved, but the primary docs show the improvement is manufactured by accounting choices and growth dilution, not structural credit healing. (1) PROVISION-COVERAGE CUT: Stage-3 PCR was deliberately cut from ~73-74% to 67.3-68% (Emkay quarterly series: 71.9% in Q3FY26 -> 67.3% in Q4FY26, -464bps QoQ), releasing Rs 290 Cr of Stage-3 management overlay. CFO Sachinn Joshi (transcript ll.840-842): the 74%-vs-68% gap "was nothing but the management overlays... released." So coverage on the remaining bad book FELL even as GS3% fell -- the opposite of conservative improvement. (2) DENOMINATOR DILUTION: consolidated loans grew +25.6% (93,773 -> 117,821). On those balances GS3% of 3.29%->2.88% implies ABSOLUTE gross Stage-3 ROSE ~+10% (~3,085 -> ~3,393 Cr) and absolute NET Stage-3 ROSE ~+24% (~910 -> ~1,131 Cr). Bad assets grew; the ratio only "improved" because the book grew faster. NS3 was effectively flat (0.97% -> 0.96%, +4bps YoY per Emkay). (3) HIDDEN CREDIT COST: the "impairment flat" story (audited 2,184.10 vs 2,193.35) understates true cost -- net loss on derecognition (amortised cost) DOUBLED to 561.77 from 275.04. Combined credit cost ROSE +11.2% (2,745.87 vs 2,468.39); on avg loans the true charge is 2.60% vs the 2.06% impairment-only figure -- a 54bps gap excluded from the "falling credit cost" narrative. The reported 2.64% quarterly credit cost also benefited from the one-off ECL refresh (Emkay/iDirect both attribute the QoQ decline to "ECL refresh" + lower slippages, not pure portfolio improvement). (4) UNSEASONED BOOK: management itself concedes (transcript ll.927-935) Cyclops "full effect... only after the book gets seasoned... full-fledged results in FY28" and Stage-1 provisioning was DOUBLED to 80bps vs RBI 40bps minimum -- an admission that incremental risk requires more, not less, provisioning. A rapidly growing, partly inorganic (gold-loan slump-sale +1,335 Cr portfolio) book mechanically depresses GS3% before slippages mature.
votes.strongest_refutation: Stage-3 PCR was CUT from ~73-74% to 67.3-68% in the same quarter GS3 "improved" -- the CFO states on the call (transcript ll.840-842) the differential "was nothing but the management overlays... released" (Rs 290 Cr). Simultaneously, on +25.6% loan growth the GS3% drop from 3.29% to 2.88% masks an ABSOLUTE rise in gross Stage-3 of ~+10% and net Stage-3 of ~+24%; and "flat impairment" (2,184.10 vs 2,193.35) excludes a DOUBLED net derecognition loss (561.77 vs 275.04), so combined credit cost actually ROSE +11.2% (2.60% vs 2.06% of avg loans). The improvement is coverage reduction + denominator dilution + reclassification, not structural healing.
votes.what_would_confirm: Audited (not management-reported) consolidated GS3/NS3 and PCR -- currently the audited pack states these are "Not Applicable at consolidated level," so only standalone GS3 2.88% is verifiable; the quarterly trajectory is sell-side/mgmt-sourced. To CONFIRM structural improvement one would need, from the annual report: (a) a Stage-3 ROLL-FORWARD/movement schedule showing gross slippages (additions to Stage 3) falling in ABSOLUTE terms and Stage-3 balances declining despite book growth; (b) PCR held or raised (not cut) while GS3 fell; (c) seasoned-vintage delinquency curves (e.g. consistent 30+/90+ DPD by origination cohort) confirming the new/Cyclops book genuinely defaults less, rather than simply being too young to have rolled into Stage 3; and (d) credit cost falling on a COMBINED basis (impairment + derecognition + ARC/assignment effects), not just the impairment line, sustained over multiple quarters without further overlay releases.

votes.pillar: Asset quality is STRUCTURALLY improving (GS3 down to 2.88%, credit cost falling)
votes.reasoning: The headline numbers are real but the word "STRUCTURALLY" and "credit cost falling" do not survive the audited data. (1) THE CREDIT-COST CLAIM IS FALSE ON A LIKE-FOR-LIKE BASIS. Consolidated impairment was effectively FLAT at 2184.10 vs FY25 2193.35 (-0.4%) despite loans growing +25.6% (93773.06->117821.03) — which superficially supports the bull. But audited p.8 shows "net loss on derecognition of financial instruments under amortised cost" DOUBLED to 561.77 from 275.04. Derecognition losses are the realized loss on loans sold/written off-balance-sheet — i.e. true credit losses routed around the impairment line. Combined true credit cost (impairment + derecognition) = 2745.87 vs 2468.39, UP +11.2% YoY in absolute terms. On avg loans the combined credit cost is ~2.60% vs an impairment-only read of ~2.06% — a hidden 54bps. So the "falling credit cost" (reported 2.64%, -19bps QoQ per emkay/idirect) is a ratio flattered by a fast-growing denominator and a metric that excludes the doubling derecognition charge. (2) THE GS3 RATIO IS NOT AUDITED AT THE RELEVANT LEVEL. Audited p.22/results note explicitly state Gross Stage 3 and Net Stage 3 are "Not Applicable" at the CONSOLIDATED level; 2.88%/0.96%/CRAR 18.34%/LCR 342% are STANDALONE only. The +25.6% loan growth and PAT belong to the consolidated entity, which has NO audited asset-quality ratio. (3) THE IMPROVEMENT IS DENOMINATOR- AND WRITE-OFF-DRIVEN, NOT STRUCTURAL CURE. Emkay's own model table shows ABSOLUTE Stage-3 loans RISING every year (GNPL Stage-3 32,180->35,110->42,292->50,199->59,646 mn) while the ratio falls only because AUM grows faster; the write-off ratio rises 0.3%->0.6%->1.0%. Management (transcript) confirms "technical write-offs" and intense collections to manage roll-forwards — GS3 is partly cleaned by charge-offs, not cures. (4) COVERAGE WAS REDUCED, NOT STRENGTHENED. Transcript: Stage-3 PCR CUT from 73% to 68% (historically 70-75%), achieved by releasing 290 Cr of Stage-3 management overlay (plus 11 Cr Stage-2) and subsuming 125 Cr macro-prudential provisions into the model. Management openly states the "actual money required per ECL model is 60-65%" and the macro-prudential buffer (peak ~975 Cr) is being run down; standalone Tier-2 subordinated debt collapsed 1833.29->702.14. Lower coverage on a still-growing absolute NPA stock, with the cushion being depleted, is the opposite of structural strengthening. Management itself flags FY27 "geopolitical headwinds." Verdict is PARTIALLY-CONFIRMED only because the standalone GS3 2.88%/NS3 0.96% and reported 2.64% are genuine and trending down, and slippage (~400 Cr/qtr, ~1.6% annualized) is genuinely lower — but the "structural" framing collapses once derecognition, the consolidated-vs-standalone gap, write-off mechanics, and overlay release are taken into account.
votes.strongest_refutation: Audited consolidated P&L (p.8): net loss on derecognition of financial instruments (amortised cost) DOUBLED to 561.77 from 275.04; combined with flat impairment (2184.10 vs 2193.35), total credit cost ROSE +11.2% YoY (2745.87 vs 2468.39) — directly contradicting "credit cost falling." The reported 2.64% decline is a denominator effect (loans +25.6%) and ignores the doubling derecognition charge (a hidden ~54bps).
votes.what_would_confirm: Annual report disclosures showing: (a) absolute Stage-3 loans DECLINING (not just the ratio) and gross slippage falling YoY in rupee terms net of write-offs; (b) a multi-year reconciliation of write-offs/derecognition so the combined credit cost (impairment + derecognition / avg loans) shows a genuine downtrend, not just the impairment line; (c) audited (not standalone-only / not management-reported) consolidated GS3/NS3 with prior-year comparatives; (d) Stage-3 PCR held or rising rather than cut from 73% to 68% via overlay release, and the macro-prudential/Tier-2 buffer being rebuilt rather than run down; (e) seasoning curves on the Cyclops-underwritten book proving lower lifetime loss, not just early low delinquency on an unseasoned vintage.

votes.reasoning: The audited and management-disclosed data show STAGNATION, not inflection, and the gap to target is widening, not closing. (1) FY26 consolidated RoA is just 2.27% on avg total assets (orchestrator-derived) / 2.39% full-year before exceptional / 2.37% after exceptional (mgmt, transcript p.~390) — DOWN 7 bps YoY after the Labour-Code charge. The 2.40% figure the bulls lean on is mgmt's Q4 exit point, up only +18 bps YoY (transcript L366) — that is a crawl, not an inflection, and still 40-53 bps below the LOW end of the 2.8% near-term band. (2) Management ADMITTED it MISSED its Lakshya-2026 RoA goal of 2.8-3.0% ('we faced certain headwinds on account of the microfinance crisis, which set us back... by a few quarters'; transcript L217-221) and merely deferred the same 2.8% to a Q4FY27 EXIT rate while stacking an even more aspirational 3.0-3.2% FY31 target on top — classic goalpost roll-forward, not delivery. (3) The entire RoA thesis rests on credit-cost decline, but on a COMBINED basis credit cost ROSE +11.2% YoY (Rs2,745.87 Cr vs Rs2,468.39 Cr; impairment 2,184.10 + net loss on derecognition 561.77 which DOUBLED from 275.04, audited p.8). 'Flat impairment' (2,184.10 vs 2,193.35) understates true credit cost and hides a 54 bps gap (2.06% impairment-only vs 2.60% combined). (4) Reported RoA is flattered by NON-RECURRING items: a Rs301 Cr ECL-overlay release with Stage-3 PCR cut from 73% to 68% (transcript L404-413), so coverage was harvested into earnings. (5) Growth was partly INORGANIC (Paul Merchants gold slump-sale Rs711.93 Cr, +Rs196 Cr goodwill), so the denominator/numerator are not clean organic. (6) Independent corroboration: I-Direct (Hold, TP Rs325) models RoA at only ~2.4% by FY27E (idirect L57-58) — i.e. NO expansion in their forecast — directly contradicting the 2.8%-by-Q4FY27 claim. Even Emkay (BUY) only calls 'gradual improvement,' contingent on a credit-cost glide-down that the combined-basis data shows is not yet happening.
votes.strongest_refutation: FY26 consolidated RoA AFTER the exceptional Labour-Code item is 2.37%, DOWN 7 bps YoY (transcript p.~390), and management itself conceded the Lakshya-2026 RoA target of 2.8-3.0% was missed and deferred to a Q4FY27 exit rate (transcript L217-221) — the audited full-year RoA (2.27% on avg total assets, orchestrator-derived) sits 53 bps BELOW the low end of even the near-term target, with the YoY move only +18 bps; that is stagnation, not inflection.
votes.what_would_confirm: Sustained (not single-quarter-exit) full-year consolidated RoA stepping above ~2.6% with the improvement driven by genuine COMBINED credit-cost decline (impairment + net loss on derecognition) toward sub-2% rather than by ECL-overlay releases or PCR reductions. votes.pillar: The AI underwriting stack (Cyclops/Nostradamus) is a DURABLE, quantified competitive edge
votes.reasoning: The pillar fails on all three tests: (1) NOT AUDITED. The audited results pack contains zero segment-level disclosure — "no separate reportable segments per Ind AS 108" (audited_results.txt L1391, L2942), and the anchor KEY DISCLOSURE FLAGS confirm GS3/NS3/CRAR are "Not Applicable at consolidated level." The headline "2.8% 30+ DPD vs 7.1% industry" exists ONLY on slide 20 of an unaudited investor presentation (transcript L174-177, L704-708), echoed by sell-side (idirect L374-376, emkay L2910-2912). It is MANAGEMENT-CLAIMED, never auditor-verified. (2) NOT QUANTIFIED at portfolio level / trivially small. By management's own admission the Cyclops observation-window book is only ~Rs3,250 Cr (transcript L707, L1041) = 2.8% of the Rs117,821 Cr consolidated loan book and ~30% of the ~Rs11,000 Cr TW book; it has run only 10 of a required 24 months and is explicitly "not a fully mature portfolio yet" (L1045-1048). Management refuses to give business-wise credit cost or LGD/PCR inputs ("ECL models are proprietary, we just can't divulge," L1207-1209), so the edge is unfalsifiable. (3) NOT DURABLE / not yet in the numbers. Full benefit is repeatedly deferred to FY28 ("full-fledged results in FY28," L927; "less than 2% trajectory by FY28... which quarter I can't point out," L994-1001). Crucially the AUDITED outcome does NOT corroborate a step-change: consolidated impairment was flat (2184.10 vs 2193.35) but the orchestrator-derived COMBINED credit cost (impairment + net loss on derecognition) actually ROSE +11.2% to 2745.87, with net loss on derecognition DOUBLING (561.77 vs 275.04) — a 54bps hidden gap. The credit-cost improvement management touts is also openly confounded by prime-mix shift (65%-to-90% prime, L982-983), aggressive collections/technical write-offs (L1169-1176), microfinance normalization (emkay L2897), and conservative ECL overlays — making any Cyclops-specific, durable contribution impossible to isolate from primary docs. This is also the FIRST cycle such metrics are disclosed (L172-173), so there is no track record to establish durability.
votes.strongest_refutation: The audited results pack carries NO segment or two-wheeler disclosure — "no separate reportable segments as per Ind AS 108" (audited_results.txt L1391/L2942) — so the only quantified proof (2.8% vs 7.1% on a Rs3,250 Cr book) is unaudited management/slide data, while the audited consolidated combined credit cost actually ROSE +11.2% YoY (2745.87 vs 2468.39) and net loss on derecognition doubled, contradicting a realized "edge."

votes.pillar: The AI underwriting stack (Cyclops/Nostradamus) is a DURABLE, quantified competitive edge
votes.reasoning: The pillar has a single, genuinely quantified data point but fails the "durable" and "audited" tests. WHAT EXISTS: one management-reported metric (transcript ~ln 704-707; mirrored in IDirect ln 374-376) that the Cyclops-underwritten two-wheeler "observation window book" shows 30+ DPD of 2.8% vs industry 7.1% at 10 months (~2.5x outperformance). Both sell-side desks (Emkay ln 23-24, IDirect ln 53-64) accept this and credit Cyclops/Nostradamus as the primary driver of guided credit-cost moderation. So the "quantified" half is partially supported on a narrow slice. WHY IT IS NOT A DURABLE, PROVEN EDGE: (1) ZERO AUDITED SUPPORT. The 2.8%/7.1% figure appears only in the investor presentation (slide 20) and transcript — it is nowhere in the SEBI audited pack. Note 11/15 (audited results ln 1391/2942) states there are "no separate reportable segments per Ind AS 108," so there is NO audited segment-level or product-level asset quality at all; the only audited asset-quality data is standalone aggregate (GS3 2.88%, NS3 0.96%). The competitive-edge claim is 100% management-claimed, unaudited. (2) IMMATERIAL, SELF-SELECTED, IMMATURE SAMPLE. The observation book is only ~Rs3,250 Cr (transcript ln 706) — ~2.8% of the Rs117,821 Cr consolidated loan book and only ~23% of the Rs14,372 Cr two-wheeler book. A 10-month vintage measured on early 30+ DPD is not full-cycle loss-tested; "durable" cannot be inferred from one cohort. (3) THE AUDITED AGGREGATE CONTRADICTS A VISIBLE "AI DIVIDEND." Impairment was flat (2184.10 vs 2193.35) but the verified COMBINED credit cost (impairment + net loss on derecognition) ROSE +11.2% to 2745.87, and net loss on derecognition DOUBLED to 561.77 (FY25 275.04). votes.pillar: The 2.6x P/B valuation is JUSTIFIED by the franchise and forward returns
votes.reasoning:
The 2.6x P/B is not justified by either current returns or credibly-delivered forward returns; it is an option premium on an inflection that is undelivered, already-slipped, and resting on low-quality FY26 earnings.

1) CURRENT RETURNS DON'T SUPPORT THE MULTIPLE. At CMP 288 and audited consol BVPS 111.74, P/B = 2.58x on an audited FY26 RoE of 11.13% (Emkay 11.1%, iDirect 10.7%). A standard Gordon franchise-value test, justified P/B = (RoE-g)/(Ke-g), with RoE 11.1%, an NBFC cost of equity ~13-14% and g 8-12%, yields a justified P/B of 0.23x-0.63x — i.e. BELOW book. Because RoE (~11%) is below Ke (~13-14%), the franchise is value-NEUTRAL-to-destructive at the margin on current economics. To justify 2.6x one must underwrite a sustainable RoE of ~19% — ABOVE management's own aspirational 16-18% Lakshya-2031 ceiling and ~1.7x what is delivered today. The multiple prices a best-case 2031 outcome as if already achieved.

2) THE BULLS' OWN MODELS CONTRADICT 'FORWARD RETURNS JUSTIFY 2.6x'. iDirect (HOLD, TP 325) explicitly anchors to ~2.3x FY28E BV and models RoE reaching only 12.6% and RoA FALLING to 2.3% by FY28E — nowhere near a level that supports 2.6x trailing. Emkay's bullish TP 330 itself implies an FY28E P/B of just 2.4x — meaning even the bull's 2-year-forward target multiple on a grown book is BELOW today's 2.6x on trailing book. Both house targets offer sub-15% upside (Emkay +14.6%, iDirect +12.8%); iDirect rates it HOLD. Neither sell-side bull argues the CURRENT 2.6x is cheap — they argue you grow into it over 2-3 years if execution holds.

3) THE RoA INFLECTION UNDERPINNING THE MULTIPLE IS UNDELIVERED AND SLIPPING. Audited FY26 RoA is 2.27% (on avg total assets; 2.82% on avg loans) vs the promised 2.8% near-term and 3.0-3.2% by 2031. iDirect states the 2.8% RoA milestone is 'delayed to Q4FY27 due to MFI crisis' (~4 quarters slip) and models RoA at 2.3-2.4% through FY28E. The re-rating thesis depends on a return level that has not printed and whose timeline already lengthened.

4) FY26 EARNINGS QUALITY UNDERCUTS THE 'FRANCHISE EARNS ITS MULTIPLE' LEG. (a) Headline 'flat impairment' (2184.10 vs 2193.35, FY25) masks a DOUBLED net loss on derecognition/write-offs (561.77 vs 275.04); combined true credit cost rose +11.2% to 2745.87, hiding 54bps (2.06% impairment-only vs 2.60% combined on avg loans). (b) Stage-3 PCR fell from 71.1% to 67.3% (Emkay -383bps YoY) into a rising unsecured mix — a coverage release that flattered Q4 PAT. (c) Treasury tailwind faded (net FV gain 183->64) and part of the +25.6% loan growth is INORGANIC (Paul Merchants gold-loan slump sale, 711.93cr, +196 goodwill). (d) Note 11/15: single-segment disclosure means NO audited segment profitability/asset quality — the franchise quality narrative is management-asserted, not audited. A multiple that pays for a clean compounding franchise is paying for an earnings stream that is, at the margin, propped by an under-reserved credit line.

Net: 2.6x P/B on ~11% RoE is a bet on a 16-19% RoE future that is promised, already deferred, and built on flattered FY26 credit metrics. The audited data refutes the claim that the multiple is currently JUSTIFIED.
